<file path="attached_assets/Pasted--Mobile-App-Fix-and-Complete-the-Holder-Wallet-Step-1-E_1781781457659.txt" lines="1-84" total_lines="84">
 Mobile App: Fix and Complete the Holder Wallet

Step 1 — Environment and API Configuration
Fix mobile/services/api/axios.js or the equivalent API client to set the base URL from EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL. Apply the same JWT token attachment and refresh logic described in the frontend web Step 1, reading the token from the mobile equivalent of the auth store using AsyncStorage or SecureStore. Ensure all API files in mobile/services/ are mapped to the same backend endpoints as the web frontend.

Step 2 — Authentication Screens
Fix the registration screen in mobile/screens/auth/RegisterScreen.jsx to implement the same three-step flow as the web: account creation, OTP verification, and national ID verification. The OTP input must be a row of six individual text inputs that auto-advance on entry. The national ID step must show the simulated OTP returned from the backend in a highlighted test mode banner during development.
Fix the login screen in mobile/screens/auth/LoginScreen.jsx to collect email and password, call POST /api/auth/login/, store tokens using SecureStore, and navigate to the main holder tabs on success.
Fix the DID auth challenge-response login in mobile/services/auth/didAuthService.js to call POST /api/did/auth/challenge/, sign the challenge with the private key from SecureStore using the Ed25519 utilities in mobile/utils/ed25519.js, and call POST /api/did/auth/respond/. Store the returned tokens and navigate to the main holder tabs on success.
Fix the biometric and PIN unlock in mobile/services/auth/localAuth.ts so that the biometric and PIN check runs on every app foreground event without calling the backend, using the locally stored session. Only call the backend when the local session has expired.

Step 3 — Credential Management Screens
Fix mobile/screens/holder/CredentialsScreen.jsx to load credentials from GET /api/wallet/credentials/ on mount and on pull-to-refresh. Store them locally using the credentialStorage service. Show a flat list of CredentialCard components. Support filter tabs for all, active, expired, and revoked. Show a skeleton loader on first load. Show an empty state if no credentials exist.
Fix mobile/screens/holder/CredentialDetailScreen.jsx to load the credential from GET /api/wallet/credentials/{id}/. Show the issuer logo, credential type, status, issue date, expiry date, and all claims in a scrollable view. Show share and present action buttons at the bottom. Show a QR code tab that generates a QR code from the credential's share token.

Step 4 — Offline Support
Fix mobile/services/offline/OfflineQueue.ts to correctly queue any failed API request when the network is unavailable, store the queue in AsyncStorage, listen for network reconnection using NetInfo, and replay all queued requests in order when connectivity returns. Log each replayed request result.
Fix mobile/services/sync/credentialSync.ts to on app foreground pull any credential updates from GET /api/wallet/credentials/ and update the local credential storage with any changed statuses or new credentials. Run this sync silently in the background without blocking the UI.

Step 5 — QR Code Features
Fix mobile/screens/holder/QrScanScreen.jsx to use the device camera to scan QR codes. On successful scan parse the QR data to determine whether it is a verification request or a share token. If it is a verification request navigate to the presentation builder with the request pre-loaded. If it is a share token navigate to the public verification result screen.
Fix mobile/screens/holder/QrDisplayScreen.jsx to accept a credential id or share token and display a large QR code. Show a save to photos button. Show a share button using the device share sheet.

Step 6 — Sharing and Presentation Screens
Fix mobile/screens/holder/ShareScreen.jsx to replicate the web sharing flow. Show the holder's credentials in a selectable list. Show claim checkboxes for selective disclosure. Show an expiry selector. On submit call POST /api/wallet/shares/ and show the returned QR code and copy link button.
Fix mobile/screens/holder/PresentationScreen.jsx to replicate the web presentation flow. Allow multi-credential selection, per-credential claim selection, and on submit call POST /api/wallet/presentations/ and display the QR code.

Step 7 — Notifications
Fix mobile/services/notifications/notificationService.ts to poll GET /api/notifications/ every sixty seconds when the app is in the foreground. Store the notifications in the local notifications state. Show a badge on the notifications tab icon with the unread count.
Fix mobile/screens/holder/NotificationsScreen.jsx to display notifications in a list. Unread items are visually highlighted. Tapping an item calls POST /api/notifications/mark-read/ and removes the highlight.

Step 8 — Settings Screen
Fix mobile/screens/holder/SettingsScreen.jsx with sections for profile, security, DID management, and app preferences.
The profile section shows name, email, and phone with an edit button that opens a bottom sheet form. On save call PUT /api/auth/me/.
The security section shows a change password option and a biometric toggle. The biometric toggle enables or disables biometric unlock using the local auth service.
The DID section shows all DIDs from GET /api/did/ and a create new DID button.
The app preferences section shows a dark mode toggle and a clear cache button.

Step 9 — DID and Crypto
Fix mobile/utils/ed25519.js to correctly implement Ed25519 key generation, signing, and verification using the available React Native crypto libraries. This must produce key pairs and signatures that are compatible with the backend's Ed25519 verification in apps/did/utils/crypto.py. Confirm compatibility by running a test where the mobile generates a key pair and signature and the backend verifies it successfully.
Fix mobile/utils/didKey.ts to generate a valid DID string in the format did:key:z followed by the base58-encoded public key, matching the format the backend creates.
Fix mobile/services/did/platformDidResolver.ts to call GET /api/did/{did}/ and return the full DID document for any given DID.

PART 4 — Integration Tests
Run each of the following tests after completing all three parts. Each test must pass completely before the work is considered done.
Test 1 — Web Registration and First Credential

Open the web app. Click Register. Complete all three registration steps including the simulated national ID verification. Confirm you are redirected to the holder dashboard. See the empty dashboard. Navigate to Request Credential. Select a credential type from a test issuer. Submit the request. Log in as the test issuer in a separate tab. Approve the request. Return to the holder tab. Refresh. Confirm the credential appears in the credentials list. Open the credential detail. Conf ...[Truncated]
Test 2 — Web Sharing and Public Verification

From the credential detail page click Share. Select two claims to disclose. Set expiry to twenty-four hours. Create the share link. Copy the link. Open it in a new incognito browser tab with no login. Confirm the public verification page loads and shows the correct result with only the two disclosed claims. Confirm the access count on the share card has incremented by one.
Test 3 — Web Revocation Handling

Log in as the test issuer. Revoke the credential issued in Test 1. Return to the holder web portal. Refresh the credentials list. Confirm the credential now shows a revoked status badge. Open the share link from Test 2 in a new tab. Confirm the public verification now shows the credential is revoked with the revocation reason.
Test 4 — Mobile Registration

Open the mobile app. Tap Register. Complete all three steps including national ID simulation. Confirm you are navigated to the main credential wallet screen. See the empty state.
Test 5 — Mobile Credential Sync

Using the test issuer issue a new credential to the holder registered in Test 4. Return to the mobile app. Pull to refresh on the credentials screen. Confirm the new credential appears. Open it and confirm all claims show correctly.
Test 6 — Mobile QR Share

From the credential detail screen on mobile tap Share. Select claims. Set expiry. Create share. Tap Display QR. Scan the QR code with another device camera. Confirm the public verification page opens in the browser on that device and shows the correct result.
Test 7 — Mobile Offline Queue

On the mobile app turn on airplane mode. Attempt to submit a credential request. Confirm the app shows an offline indicator and queues the action. Turn airplane mode off. Confirm the sync indicator appears and the queued request is submitted automatically. Log in as the issuer and confirm the request arrived.
Test 8 — DID Authentication on Mobile

From the mobile settings create a new DID. Log out. On the login screen choose Sign in with DID. Select the created DID. Confirm the challenge-response flow completes and you are logged back in.

Rules to Follow Throughout
Every API call in the frontend and mobile must handle loading, success, and error states. No silent failures are acceptable anywhere.
Every form must validate on the client using Zod or equivalent before any API call is made. Backend validation errors must be displayed next to the relevant field.
The national ID simulation must return the test OTP in the response during development. This behavior must be controlled by a flag in the backend settings so it can be turned off in production without changing any code.
The Ed25519 implementation on mobile must produce output that the backend can verify. Test this explicitly.
The offline queue must never submit a request more than once. Use a unique request id on each queued item to prevent duplicates on replay.
Tokens must be stored securely. The mobile app must use Expo SecureStore, not AsyncStorage, for access and refresh tokens.
All three layers must read their backend URL from an environment variable. No URL must be hardcoded anywhere in the codebase.
If a fix in one layer requires a change in another layer, make both changes in the same step and confirm both sides work before moving on.

How to Work
Complete Part 1 entirely before starting Part 2. Complete Part 2 entirely before starting Part 3. Run the integration tests in Part 4 only after all three parts are complete.
After each step within a part, confirm the specific functionality fixed in that step works before moving to the next step. Do not accumulate broken steps.
Report after each part with a clear summary of what was fixed, what endpoints were added or corrected, what screens were updated, and any issues found that need attention in a later part.
</file>
